nodes.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Point artefact links to resolvable pages**

The new hero links use directory URLs (`artefacts/` and `artefacts/canary-harness/`), but this commit only adds Markdown files inside those directories (`README.md`, etc.) and no `index.html` pages. On static hosting (including typical GitHub Pages setups), directory URLs without an index file return 404, so readers cannot open the promised artefact bundle from the report. Please link directly to concrete files like `artefacts/README.md` (and `artefacts/canary-harness/README.md`) or add index pages for those directories.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Link artefact chips to concrete files**

The new hero chip uses a directory URL (`href="artefacts/"`), but this commit only adds `README.md` inside that directory and no `index.html`/`index.md`, so static hosting will not resolve this path as a page and readers will hit a dead link instead of the artefact bundle. This re-raises the earlier 404 concern with fresh evidence: the same unresolved pattern is now present in the POC report as well (not just the memory report), so the regression affects multiple published pages.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

### CSS specificity causes violet text instead of teal on artefact chips **Medium Severity** The new `.rs-section a.rs-mono` rule (specificity 0,2,1) omits a `color` declaration. For the `color` property, `.rs-section a` (specificity 0,1,1, setting `var(--dx-violet)`) beats `.rs-mono` (specificity 0,1,0, setting `var(--dx-teal)`). All `a.rs-mono` artefact-chip links inside `.rs-section` render with violet text on a teal-tinted background/border — visually inconsistent with regular `.rs-mono` code tokens. The PR verification claims "§04 `methodology →` rendered as teal mono chip" but the cascade produces violet text instead.
 
Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 1d86abea1a35cc314bd08351c9a03816c0aabbd7. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).

Review Summary by Qodo

v22 — Reposition to independent practitioner framing + publish sub-artefact bundle 📝 Documentation ✨ Enhancement 🐞 Bug fix

Walkthroughs

Description 
  **Positioning reframe from institutional to independent practitioner framing:**
• Removed EPAM institutional attribution from all bylines, page titles, and meta descriptions across
  landing page and both reports
• Updated eyebrow tags: "Substrate study" → "Applied research note", "POC report" → "Engineering
  brief"
• Softened institutional language throughout prose (e.g., "EPAM accounts" → "enterprise-agent
  engagements", "EPAM delivery teams" → "delivery teams")
• Added explicit "not an official EPAM publication" disclaimers on landing page and in new
  ABOUT.md
• Rewritten README.md with H1 "Applied AI Research — practitioner notes" and clarified
  Contributing section for practitioners
  **Sub-artefact bundle with 14 new documentation files:**
• **POC report** (2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/): README.md, methodology.md,
  claims.md, invalidation.md, telemetry-schema.md,
  pricing-snapshot/bedrock-us-east-ohio-2026-05-14.md
• **Memory report** (2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/): README.md,
  methodology.md, claims.md, invalidation.md, adoption-bar.md, task-families.md,
  canary-harness/README.md, canary-harness/schema.md, canary-harness/example-fixture.jsonl
• Each artefact documents methodology, claims ledger, falsification conditions, and intentional
  exclusions (held-out fixtures, proprietary code, deployment-specific data)
• Closes the §11 "publishes the canary harness" promise with reproducible protocol and example
  fixtures
  **Inline linking and styling:**
• Added 18 inline links from report prose to sub-artefact files with .rs-mono chip styling
• Added hero-meta artefacts chips linking to artefacts/ directories
• New CSS rule .rs-section a.rs-mono for chip-style link appearance with hover effects
  **Bug fixes (cherry-picked from PR #11):**
• Global box-sizing: border-box reset fixing 63.5px topbar misalignment caused by main
  content-box + right padding interaction
• Topbar and pill styling corrections
  **Intentional exclusions:** runnable harness code, results CSV bundles, real task
  prompts/validators, adapter source code, per-trial transcripts (all documented in artefact README
  files with rationale)
Diagram
```mermaid flowchart LR A["Institutional byline
EPAM attribution"] -->|"Remove EPAM
from surfaces"| B["Independent practitioner
framing"] C["Report prose
without artefacts"] -->|"Link to 14 new
supporting files"| D["Artefact bundle
methodology + claims
+ invalidation"] E["Topbar misalignment
box-sizing issue"] -->|"Global border-box
reset"| F["Aligned layout
+ chip styling"] B --> G["Landing + 2 reports
repositioned"] D --> G F --> G ```

File Changes

>

View more (18)
4. assets/dialx/research.css 🐞 Bug fix +20/-0
>Add box-sizing reset and artefact-chip link styles >
>• Added global box-sizing: border-box reset to fix 63.5px topbar misalignment caused by main
>  content-box + right padding interaction
>• Added new CSS rule .rs-section a.rs-mono for inline artefact-chip link styling with hover
>  background effect
>

5. 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/methodology.md 📝 Documentation +130/-0
>Document memory-substrate evaluation methodology and re-instantiation pattern >
>• New file documenting the paired-question protocol, two-judge position-swap fixture, pre-registered
>  rule-change threshold, and frequency-weighting derivation
>• Specifies 8 task families with production weights, 64 paired questions, 128 paired trial
>  appearances per substrate
>• Details the 5-label scoring rubric (correct, partial, incorrect, abstained,
>  hallucinated) and position-swap collapse rule
>• Includes re-instantiation checklist for practitioners to reproduce the canary on their own corpus
>
> > 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/methodology.md
6. 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/methodology.md 📝 Documentation +104/-0
>Document POC evaluation methodology and adapter conformance shape >
>• New file specifying the 8-task portfolio shape, two-wave evaluation structure (120 + 72 trials),
>  adapter conformance contract, and cost formula
>• Documents the pass criterion (zero validator failures within 15-turn loop), aggregation formula
>  for $/success, and pricing source (AWS Bedrock list rates, 2026-05-14)
>• Includes adapter conformance shape (Anthropic ingress → OpenAI egress, SSE handling, per-request
>  telemetry)
>• Provides re-instantiation guidance for practitioners to reproduce the evaluation on their own
>  gateway
>
> > 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/methodology.md
7. 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/claims.md 📝 Documentation +39/-0
>Publish claims ledger mapping findings to evidence and scope >
>• New file tagging every headline claim by category: measured, inferred, hypothetical,
>  recommendation
>• Maps 18 claims to evidence rows, scope qualifiers, and strength of claim
>• Distinguishes direct observations from the 192 trials vs inferred conclusions vs illustrative
>  scenarios vs deployment recommendations
>• Provides guidance for external citation: quote measured rows with scope, inferred rows as
>  interpretations, hypothetical rows only as scenarios
>
> > 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/claims.md
8. 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/telemetry-schema.md 📝 Documentation +108/-0
>Document adapter telemetry schema and three-layer field taxonomy >
>• New file documenting the ~30 fields the adapter writes on every response_out, separated into
>  three layers (A: deployed today, B: mechanical to wire, C: requires headers)
>• Specifies Layer A fields (model_id, target_model_family, client_name, tokens, cost, latency,
>  stop_reason) reaching InfluxDB and Grafana today
>• Documents Layer B fields (session.id, tool_inventory_hash, mcp_servers_seen) that exist in JSON
>  log but need tag promotion
>• Details Layer C fields (project_code, team_id, industry_vertical, use_case_label) requiring
>  business-identity headers and data-governance policy
>• Includes wire format (JSONL), sidecar exporter architecture, and cardinality concerns
>
> > 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/telemetry-schema.md
9. 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/claims.md 📝 Documentation +36/-0
>Publish claims ledger for memory-substrate findings and scope limits >
>• New file tagging 15 headline claims by category: measured, inferred, hypothetical,
>  recommendation, process, scope
>• Maps claims to evidence pointers (128 paired trials, closed-loop pilot, public-source review) with
>  scope qualifiers
>• Distinguishes the 36-point LightRAG-vs-Graphiti gap as driven by production-weighting, not
>  substrate superiority
>• Flags the 0-vs-4 hallucination count as structural-class indicator, not statistical claim
>• Provides external citation guidance and emphasizes that the durable artefact is the canary harness
>  shape, not the magnitudes
>
> > 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/claims.md
10. 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/canary-harness/README.md 📝 Documentation +100/-0
>Publish canary harness protocol as re-instantiable durable artefact >
>• New file documenting the durable artefact: the paired-question protocol, position-swap judging
>  fixture, 5-label scoring rubric, and pre-registered threshold
>• Specifies the run sequence: retrieve from both substrates, judge in both orderings, collapse ties,
>  aggregate per family
>• Provides re-instantiation checklist for practitioners to reproduce on their own corpus in a day
>• Explains why runnable code is not shipped (validators are held-out fixture; deployment wiring is
>  specific)
>• Emphasizes that the protocol shape transfers; the specific magnitudes do not
>
> > 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/canary-harness/README.md
11. 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/canary-harness/schema.md 📝 Documentation +89/-0
>Define canary harness JSONL input/output schema for paired questions >
>• New file specifying JSONL input/output schemas for paired-question fixture and judged-record
>  output
>• Documents input fields: id, family, query, expected_label, expected_doc_id, notes
>• Documents output fields: question_id, family, judge, ordering, substrate_a_id,
>  substrate_b_id, retrieval_a, retrieval_b, winner, label_a, label_b, judge_rationale,
>  elapsed_ms, cost_usd_estimate
>• Includes position-swap collapse rule, aggregation reference, hallucination counting formula, and
>  schema versioning note
>
> > 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/canary-harness/schema.md
12. 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/pricing-snapshot/bedrock-us-east-ohio-2026-05-14.md 📝 Documentation +81/-0
>Publish AWS Bedrock pricing snapshot for cost-per-success derivation >
>• New file capturing AWS Bedrock list pricing snapshot (US East Ohio, 2026-05-14) for all 8 models
>  in the POC matrix
>• Transcribes $/M input and output rates for Claude Haiku/Sonnet/Opus, Qwen3-Coder 480B/30B, Kimi
>  K2.5, MiniMax M2.5, DeepSeek v3.2
>• Documents the cost formula: total_bedrock_spend / N_pass = $/success
>• Includes markup-stability sensitivity analysis: ranking stable under ~6.7× markup on Qwen input
>  rates
>• Provides Wayback Machine URL for cross-checking against future pricing changes
>
> > 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/pricing-snapshot/bedrock-us-east-ohio-2026-05-14.md
13. README.md 📝 Documentation +13/-14
>Reframe repository README to independent practitioner positioning >
>• Rewritten H1 from "Applied AI Research · EPAM" to "Applied AI Research — practitioner notes"
>• Updated description to emphasize independent status and real-stack measurement discipline
>• Added "What 'applied AI research' means here" section explaining engineering decision memos vs
>  academic research
>• Updated Contributing section to invite practitioners working on similar problems, removed
>  EPAM-specific framing
>• Updated License section to clarify CC BY 4.0 for reports/artefacts, MIT for code, and EPAM
>  trademark ownership
>• Added reference to ABOUT.md for full disclaimer
>
> > README.md
14. 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/task-families.md 📝 Documentation +58/-0
>Production-weighted task families and canary methodology >
>• Introduces the 8 task families used in the retrieval canary with production-frequency weighting
>  (50% current-canonical-text, 20% handoff-continuity, 10% entity-disambiguation, 4% each for
>  temporal-supersession, multi-hop-traversal, knowledge-updates, abstention-on-false-premise,
>  link-graph-traversal)
>• Documents the production-weighting derivation methodology based on 6 weeks of actual agent traffic
>  (~2,400 queries) to avoid circular reasoning
>• Explains why production weighting drives the 36-point gap finding and how uniform weighting would
>  show statistical tie between substrates
>• Provides guidance for re-deriving weights on different corpus classes (customer-support, legal,
>  medical) with a 5-step methodology
>
> > 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/task-families.md
15. 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/invalidation.md 📝 Documentation +45/-0
>Falsification conditions and invalidation ledger for findings >
>• Specifies Popperian falsification conditions for each headline finding with concrete
>  re-measurement criteria
>• Documents invalidation thresholds for retrieval-gap finding (20pp threshold), reliability finding
>  (hallucination counts), pilot stale-fact inversion, and Tencent DB Agent-Memory adoption criteria
>• Establishes that bar and methodology are durable artefacts while specific magnitudes expire with
>  model/corpus/implementation changes
>• Encourages structured push-back via GitHub issues with new measurements rather than debate
>
> > 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/invalidation.md
16. 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/adoption-bar.md 📝 Documentation +46/-0
>Reproducible adoption criteria and substrate evaluation rubric >
>• Defines five-criterion rubric for evaluating memory-substrate candidates: integration-surface
>  (0.25 weight), install-hygiene (0.20), test-coverage (0.20), maintenance-posture (0.15),
>  operational-fit (0.20)
>• Renders Tencent DB Agent-Memory scorecard showing 4 fails and 1 partial against the stated bar
>• Explains that weights are calibrated to specific delivery context (Python-first stack, EPAM DIAL
>  gateway, MCP-compatible agents) and should be restated for different contexts
>• Provides reusable methodology for applying the bar to different candidates with version tracking
>  and evidence documentation
>
> > 2026-05-19_choosing-memory-for-enterprise-agents/artefacts/adoption-bar.md
17. 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/invalidation.md 📝 Documentation +42/-0
>POC report falsification conditions and methodology commitments >
>• Specifies falsification conditions for POC headline findings: Qwen3-Coder 480B cost-leader claim
>  (re-run at same pricing, different harness), sub-Sonnet OSS replacement claim (20+ pass threshold,
>  criterion relaxation), scale-vs-vendor failure signature (fifth vendor family success, architectural
>  variable identification)
>• Documents invalidation for adapter telemetry plane claim (DIAL ingress constraints) and Layer C
>  per-tenant analysis ask (data-governance policy, cardinality explosion)
>• Establishes methodology principle that bar and criteria are durable while magnitudes expire with
>  model/price/gateway changes
>
> > 2026-05-15_claude-code-via-dial-poc/artefacts/invalidation.md
18. ABOUT.md 📝 Documentation +45/-0
>Series editorial independence and research methodology statement >
>• Establishes authorship by Arseny Gorokh as independent practitioner at EPAM Applied AI with
>  explicit disclaimer that this is not official EPAM publication

rest_inline.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Point artefact links to resolvable pages**

The new hero links use directory URLs (`artefacts/` and `artefacts/canary-harness/`), but this commit only adds Markdown files inside those directories (`README.md`, etc.) and no `index.html` pages. On static hosting (including typical GitHub Pages setups), directory URLs without an index file return 404, so readers cannot open the promised artefact bundle from the report. Please link directly to concrete files like `artefacts/README.md` (and `artefacts/canary-harness/README.md`) or add index pages for those directories.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

rest_inline.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Link artefact chips to concrete files**

The new hero chip uses a directory URL (`href="artefacts/"`), but this commit only adds `README.md` inside that directory and no `index.html`/`index.md`, so static hosting will not resolve this path as a page and readers will hit a dead link instead of the artefact bundle. This re-raises the earlier 404 concern with fresh evidence: the same unresolved pattern is now present in the POC report as well (not just the memory report), so the regression affects multiple published pages.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

### CSS specificity causes violet text instead of teal on artefact chips **Medium Severity** The new `.rs-section a.rs-mono` rule (specificity 0,2,1) omits a `color` declaration. For the `color` property, `.rs-section a` (specificity 0,1,1, setting `var(--dx-violet)`) beats `.rs-mono` (specificity 0,1,0, setting `var(--dx-teal)`). All `a.rs-mono` artefact-chip links inside `.rs-section` render with violet text on a teal-tinted background/border — visually inconsistent with regular `.rs-mono` code tokens. The PR verification claims "§04 `methodology →` rendered as teal mono chip" but the cascade produces violet text instead.
